title: Tapping for Self-Forgiveness
subtitle: An EFT sequence for releasing the resentment held against yourself
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the resentment directed inward: the things you did that you haven't forgiven yourself for, the standard of conduct you failed to meet, the version of yourself you keep trying. Works with self-criticism toward something that allows you to continue.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you are holding something you did against yourself, particularly if you have been carrying it for longer than is useful.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for self-forgiveness. The script works with the specific things held against yourself: the choices, failures, and lapses you keep returning to, and moves toward the point where you are allowed to continue.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I did something I am still holding against myself, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the inner critic keeps bringing this back and I haven't found a way to put it down, I accept what I feel right now.
Even though forgiving myself feels like letting myself off too easily, I am open to the possibility that I am allowed to continue.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The thing I did that I keep returning to.
Side of Eye: The version of me who did it and what I knew then.
Under Eye: The feeling that I should have known better.
Under Nose: The inner critic that keeps the evidence file open.
Chin: Holding myself to a standard I didn't have the resources to meet.
Collarbone: The weight of being my own worst witness.
Under Arm: What keeping this serves.
Top of Head: The possibility of putting this down.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I did what I did with what I had at the time.
Side of Eye: The self-criticism has noted the error. It does not need to run forever.
Under Eye: I am not required to keep punishing myself as proof that I care.
Under Nose: The better version of me that this event helped create is here now.
Chin: I can carry the lesson without carrying the sentence.
Collarbone: I forgive myself for being human and limited.
Under Arm: I am allowed to continue from here.
Top of Head: I put this down. I move forward.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
